sections.name: Chemical and Physical Foundations of Biological Systems

foundational_concepts.name: Complex living organisms transport materials, sense their environment, process signals, and respond to changes using processes that can be understood in terms of physical principles.

content_categories.name: Translational motion, forces, work, energy, and equilibrium in living systems

children.name: Newton’s Third Law, forces equal and opposite
Vector analysis of forces acting on a point object
Work done by a constant force: W = Fd cosθ
Transverse and longitudinal waves: wavelength and propagation speed

content_categories.name: Importance of fluids for the circulation of blood, gas movement, and gas exchange

children.name: Hydrostatic pressure; P = ρgh (pressure vs. depth)
Concept of turbulence at high velocities
Arterial and venous systems; pressure and flow characteristics
Molar volume at 0°C and 1 atm = 22.4 L/mol
Heat capacity at constant volume and at constant pressure
Deviation of real gas behavior from ideal gas law
Dalton’s Law relating partial pressure to composition

content_categories.name: Electrochemistry and electrical circuits and their elements

children.name: Electrostatic energy, electric potential at a point in space
Current I = ΔQ/Δt , sign conventions, units
Motion of charged particles in magnetic fields; Lorentz Force
Faraday’s Law relating amount of elements deposited (or gas liberated) at an electrode to current
Electron flow; oxidation, and reduction at the electrodes
Myelin sheath, Schwann cells, insulation of axon
Nodes of Ranvier: propagation of nerve impulse along axon

content_categories.name: How light and sound interact with matter

children.name: Relative speed of sound in solids, liquids, and gases
Intensity of sound, decibel units, log scale
Doppler Effect: moving sound source or observer, reflection of sound from a moving object
Concept of Interference; Young Double-slit Experiment
Thin films, diffraction grating, single-slit diffraction
Polarization of light: linear and circular
Velocity equals constant c, in vacuo
Electromagnetic radiation consists of perpendicularly oscillating electric and magnetic fields; direction of propagation is perpendicular to both
Classification of electromagnetic spectrum, photon energy E = hf
Recognizing common characteristic group absorptions, fingerprint region
Absorption in visible region gives complementary color (e.g., carotene)
Effect of structural changes on absorption (e.g., indicators)
Protons in a magnetic field; equivalent protons
Reflection from plane surface: angle of incidence equals angle of reflection
Refraction, refractive index n; Snell’s law: n 1 sin θ1 = n 2 sin θ2
Dispersion, change of index of refraction with wavelength
Use of formula 1/p + 1/q = 1/f , with sign conventions
Optical Instruments, including the human eye

content_categories.name: Atoms, nuclear decay, electronic structure, and atomic chemical behavior

children.name: Orbital structure of hydrogen atom, principal quantum number n, number of electrons per orbital

topics.name: The Periodic Table – Classification of Elements into Groups by Electronic Structure

children.name: Alkaline earth metals: their chemical characteristics
Noble gases: their physical and chemical characteristics

children.name: Prediction from electronic structure for elements in different groups or rows
Comparative values for some representative elements and important groups
Electron shells and the sizes of atoms
Electron shells and the sizes of ions
Metric units commonly used in the context of chemistry
Description of composition by percent mass
Description of reactions by chemical equations

foundational_concepts.name: The principles that govern chemical interactions and reactions form the basis for a broader understanding of the molecular dynamics of living systems.

content_categories.name: Unique nature of water and its solutions

children.name: Kw, its approximate value (Kw = [H+ ][OH– ] = 10^(–14) at 25°C, 1 atm)
Definition of pH: pH of pure water
Conjugate acids and bases (e.g., NH4+ and NH3)
Strong acids and bases (e.g., nitric, sulfuric)
Weak acids and bases (e.g., acetic, benzoic)
Dissociation of weak acids and bases with or without added salt
Hydrolysis of salts of weak acids or bases
Calculation of pH of solutions of salts of weak acids or bases
Equilibrium Constants Ka and Kb: pKa, pKb
Anion, cation: common names, formulas and charges for familiar ions (e.g., NH4+ ammonium, PO4 3– phosphate, SO4 2– sulfate)
Solubility product constant; the equilibrium expression Ksp
Common ion effect, its use in laboratory separations

content_categories.name: Nature of molecules and intermolecular interactions

children.name: Role of electronegativity in determining charge distribution
Hybrid orbitals: sp3 , sp2 , sp and respective geometries
Valence shell electron pair repulsion and the prediction of shapes of molecules (e.g., NH3, H2O, CO2)
Structural formulas for molecules involving H, C, N, O, F, S, P, Si, Cl

Effect on bond length and bond energies
Conventions for writing R and S forms
Conventions for writing E and Z forms
Van der Waals’ Forces (London dispersion forces)

content_categories.name: Separation and purification methods

children.name: Extraction: distribution of solute between two immiscible solvents
Chromatography: Basic principles involved in separation process

content_categories.name: Structure, function, and reactivity of biologically-relevant molecules

children.name: Absolute configuration at the α position
Sulfur linkage for cysteine and cystine
Cyclic structure and conformations of hexoses
Nucleophilic addition reactions at C=O bond
Reactions at adjacent positions: enolate chemistry
Effect of substituents on reactivity of C=O; steric hindrance
Amides (and lactam), esters (and lactone), anhydride formation
Oxidation and reduction (hydroquinones, ubiquinones): biological 2e- redox centers

content_categories.name: Principles of chemical thermodynamics and kinetics

children.name: Effects of local conditions on enzyme activity

topics.name: Energy Changes in Chemical Reactions – Thermochemistry, Thermodynamics

children.name: Dependence of reaction rate on concentration of reactants

sections.name: Critical Analysis and Reasoning Skills
sections._notes: CARS has no Foundational Concept or Content Category structure. It tests critical analysis and reasoning skills on humanities and social-science passages. AAMC does not publish a topic list for CARS.

skill_categories.name: Foundations of Comprehension
skill_categories.description: Understanding the basic components of the text

skill_categories.name: Reasoning Within the Text
skill_categories.description: Integrating distant passage components into a generalized interpretation

skill_categories.name: Reasoning Beyond the Text
skill_categories.description: Applying or extrapolating ideas from the passage to new contexts

sections.name: Biological and Biochemical Foundations of Living Systems

foundational_concepts.name: Biomolecules have unique properties that determine how they contribute to the structure and function of cells and how they participate in the processes necessary to maintain life.

content_categories.name: Structure and function of proteins and their constituent amino acids

children.name: Absolute configuration at the α position
Sulfur linkage for cysteine and cystine
3° structure of proteins; role of proline, cystine, hydrophobic bonding
Function of enzymes in catalyzing biological reactions
Effects of local conditions on enzyme activity

content_categories.name: Transmission of genetic information from the gene to the protein

children.name: Deoxyribonucleic acid (DNA): double helix, Watson–Crick model of DNA structure
Base pairing specificity: A with T, G with C
Function in transmission of genetic information
Mechanism of replication: separation of strands, specific coupling of free nucleic acids
Origins of replication, multiple origins in eukaryotes
Replicating the ends of DNA molecules
mRNA processing in eukaryotes, introns, exons
Ribozymes, spliceosomes, small nuclear ribonucleoproteins (snRNPs), small nuclear RNAs (snRNAs)

Practical applications of DNA technology: medical applications, human gene therapy, pharmaceuticals, forensic evidence, environmental cleanup, agriculture

content_categories.name: Transmission of heritable information from generation to generation and the processes that increase genetic diversity

Evolutionary success as increase in percent representation in the gene pool of the next generation

children.name: Relationship of the equilibrium constant and ΔG°

children.name: Higher level integration of hormone structure and function

foundational_concepts.name: Highly organized assemblies of molecules, cells, and organs interact to carry out the functions of living organisms.

content_categories.name: Assemblies of molecules, cells, and groups of cells within single cellular and multicellular organisms

foundational_concepts.name: Complex systems of tissues and organs sense the internal and external environments of multicellular organisms, and through integrated functioning, maintain a stable internal environment within an ever-changing external environment.

sections.name: Psychological, Social, and Biological Foundations of Behavior

foundational_concepts.name: Biological, psychological, and sociocultural factors influence the ways that individuals perceive, think about, and react to the world.

content_categories.name: Sensing the environment

children.name: Structure and function of the eye
Structure and function of the ear
Auditory processing (e.g., auditory pathways in the brain)
Taste (e.g., taste buds/chemoreceptors that detect specific chemicals)
Olfactory cells/chemoreceptors that detect specific chemicals
Perceptual organization (e.g., depth, form, motion, constancy)
